info.title: Global Expansion Camp API Reference Documentation

get.description:
Retrieve a list of currently available models.

The response format is automatically identified based on the request headers:
- Returns the Anthropic format when both `x-api-key` and `anthropic-version` headers are included
- Returns the Gemini format when the `x-goog-api-key` header or the `key` query parameter is included
- Returns the OpenAI format in all other cases
Returns a list of available models in Gemini API format

post.description:
Creates model responses based on conversation history. Supports both streaming and non-streaming responses.

Compatible with OpenAI Chat Completions API.

OpenAI Responses API, used to create model responses.
Supports multi-turn conversations, tool calling, reasoning, and other features.

Gemini image generation.

The Nano Banana series supports the OpenAI Dall-E format (see [Image Generation OpenAI DALL-E Format](#tag/%E5%9B%BE%E7%89%87%E7%94%9F%E6%88%90OpenAI%20DALL-E%E6%A0%BC%E5%BC%8F) for details).

Add field descriptions for Gemini image generation in /v1beta/models/{model}:generateContent to prevent the public OpenAPI from losing descriptions on nested fields.

role.description: Message role. Common values are `user` or `model`.

inlineData.description: An inline binary content object for inputs such as base64 images.

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the inline content, for example `image/png`.

data.description: The binary data content after Base64 encoding.

parts.description: Array of message segments; text, images, and other inputs are placed here.

contents.description: Required. Array of Gemini input content, typically containing at least one user message.

responseModalities.description: List of response modes; image generation usually passes `IMAGE`.

imageSize.description: Output resolution level, for example `1K`, `2K`, and `4K`.

post.description:
OpenAI DALL-E format image generation API.

- `POST /v1/images/generations`: Create an image generation task

Standard authentication headers:
- `Content-Type: application/json`

- `model: string`: Required, public model name
- `prompt: string`: Recommended, image generation prompt
- `n: number`: Optional, number of images to generate
- `size: string`: Optional, size or resolution specification
- `quality: string`: Optional, quality level
- `response_format: string`: Optional, response format

Common compatible extension parameters:
- `image`
- `image_url`
- `image_urls`
- `image_list`
- `subject_image_list`
- `scene_image`
- `style_image`
- `negative_prompt`
- `aspect_ratio`
- `resolution`
- `callback_url`
- `external_task_id`

Kling-specific extension parameters:
- `model_name`
- `element_list`
- `result_type`
- `series_amount`
- `human_image`
- `cloth_image`
- `up_expansion_ratio`
- `down_expansion_ratio`
- `left_expansion_ratio`
- `right_expansion_ratio`

### 3.1 Nano Banana Series

Public model:
- `nano-banana`
- `nano-banana-pro`
- `nano-banana-pro-2k`
- `nano-banana-pro-4k`
- `nano-banana-2`
- `nano-banana-2-2k`
- `nano-banana-2-4k`

Capabilities:
- `nano-banana`: Basic text-to-image, also compatible with image editing
- `nano-banana-pro*`: Higher-quality version
- `nano-banana-2*`: Newer model family
- `*-2k` / `*-4k`: High-resolution variants

Common parameters:
- Text-to-image: `model + prompt`
- Image editing: `model + prompt + image`, also compatible with `image_urls`
- Common extensions: `n` `size` `quality` `response_format`

Example:

```json
{
"model": "nano-banana",
"prompt": "a cute cat running in cloud",
"n": 1,
"size": "1024x1792"
}
```

```json
{
"model": "nano-banana-pro",
"prompt": "把主体改成赛博朋克夜景",
"image": "https://example.com/input.png",
"n": 1
}
```

### 3.2 Seedream Series

Public model:
- `doubao-seedream-3-0-t2i-250415`
- `doubao-seedream-4-0-250828`
- `doubao-seedream-4-5-251128`
- `doubao-seedream-5-0-260128`

Capabilities:
- Use the text-to-image call uniformly
- It is recommended to pass the standard parameter first: `prompt + model + size/aspect_ratio`

Common parameters:
- `model + prompt`
- Optional: `size` `aspect_ratio` `n`

### 3.3 Kling Standard Image Base Model

Public base model:
- `kling-image`

Required:
- `model`
- `model_name`

Supported `model_name`:
- `kling-v1`
- `kling-v1-5`
- `kling-v2`
- `kling-v2-new`
- `kling-v2-1`
- `kling-v3`

Capabilities:
- Text-to-image: `model + model_name + prompt`
- Image-to-image: add `image` on top of text-to-image
- Multi-image generation: pass `subject_image_list`, or pass multiple `image_urls/images`
- Outpainting: pass `image` and the outpainting ratio parameter

Common parameters:
- `image`
- `subject_image_list`
- `scene_image`
- `style_image`
- `aspect_ratio`
- `resolution`
- `up_expansion_ratio`
- `down_expansion_ratio`
- `left_expansion_ratio`
- `right_expansion_ratio`

Example:

```json
{
"model": "kling-image",
"model_name": "kling-v3",
"prompt": "海边日落，电影感人像",
"aspect_ratio": "16:9",
"n": 1
}
```

### 3.4 Kling Omni Image Base Model

Public base model:
- `kling-omni-image`

Required:
- `model`
- `model_name`

Supported `model_name`:
- `kling-image-o1`
- `kling-v3-omni`

Common parameters:
- `image_list`
- `element_list`
- `resolution`
- `aspect_ratio`
- `result_type`
- `series_amount`

Example:

```json
{
"model": "kling-omni-image",
"model_name": "kling-v3-omni",
"prompt": "生成一张海边跳舞的图片",
"element_list": [
{ "element_id": 835266081714884677 }
]
}
```

### 3.5 Kling Virtual Try-On Model

Public model:
- `kolors-virtual-try-on-v1`
- `kolors-virtual-try-on-v1-5`

Minimum input parameters:
- `model + human_image + cloth_image`

Description:
- These two models are already complete primary models themselves, so there is no need to pass `model_name` again.

Example:

```json
{
"model": "kolors-virtual-try-on-v1",
"human_image": "https://example.com/human.jpg",
"cloth_image": "https://example.com/cloth.jpg"
}
```

prompt.description: Image generation description. This can be omitted for the virtual try-on model.

value.prompt: a cute cat running in cloud
一张电影感海边日落人像
海边日落，电影感人像
改成赛博朋克夜景风格
生成一张海边跳舞的图片

post.description: OpenAI Dall-E format image editing API.

Video generation interface compatible with OpenAI Sora. Supports text-to-video and image/video reference generation modes.

- `POST /v1/videos`: Create video task
- `GET /v1/videos/{video_id}`: Query task status
- `GET /v1/videos/{video_id}/content`: Retrieve the video binary content

Currently not available externally:
- `GET /v1/videos`
- `POST /v1/videos/{video_id}/remix`

Standard authentication headers:
- `Content-Type: application/json`

1. Use `POST /v1/videos` to create a task and obtain `id`
2. Poll `GET /v1/videos/{id}` until `completed` or `failed`
3. After completion, prefer using `video_url` returned in the response
4. For unified download, call `GET /v1/videos/{id}/content`

Core parameters:
- `model: string`: Required, public model name
- `prompt: string`: Recommended, video generation description
- `seconds: number|string`: Optional, target duration
- `duration: number|string`: Optional, duration alias
- `size: string`: Optional, output resolution
- `mode: string`: Optional, common values: `t2v` `i2v` `i2v_first_last` `reference_material`

Common compatibility parameters:
- `aspect_ratio`
- `ratio`
- `quality`
- `resolution`
- `fps`
- `image`
- `image_url`
- `image_urls`
- `images`
- `reference_images`
- `input_reference`
- `end_image_url`
- `last_image_url`
- `video_urls`
- `audio_urls`
- `function_mode`
- `content`
- `callback_url`
- `external_task_id`

Common extended parameters for Kling:
- `model_name`
- `negative_prompt`
- `cfg_scale`
- `sound`
- `camera_control`
- `image_list`
- `video_id`
- `task_id`
- `watermark_info`

- If you have fixed requirements for resolution, orientation, or duration, prefer preset model names
- For image-to-video or reference-to-video scenarios, it is recommended to explicitly pass `mode + image_url/reference_images`

### 4.2 Sora Series

Base models:
- `sora-2`
- `sora-2-pro`
- `sora-2-stable`
- `sora-2-pro-stable`
- `sora-2-official`
- `sora-2-pro-official`

Preset models:
- `sora2-landscape-10s`
- `sora2-landscape-10s-stable`
- `sora2-landscape-10s-official`
- `sora2-portrait-10s`
- `sora2-portrait-10s-stable`
- `sora2-portrait-10s-official`
- `sora2-landscape-15s`
- `sora2-landscape-15s-stable`
- `sora2-landscape-15s-official`
- `sora2-portrait-15s`
- `sora2-portrait-15s-stable`
- `sora2-portrait-15s-official`
- `sora2-pro-landscape-25s`
- `sora2-pro-landscape-25s-stable`
- `sora2-pro-landscape-25s-official`
- `sora2-pro-portrait-25s`
- `sora2-pro-portrait-25s-stable`
- `sora2-pro-portrait-25s-official`
- `sora2-pro-landscape-hd-15s`
- `sora2-pro-landscape-hd-15s-stable`
- `sora2-pro-landscape-hd-15s-official`
- `sora2-pro-portrait-hd-15s`
- `sora2-pro-portrait-hd-15s-stable`
- `sora2-pro-portrait-hd-15s-official`

Recommendations:
- `sora-2*` is suitable for general-purpose use
- The preset model `sora2-*` is suitable for scenarios with fixed duration and orientation
- If you need stable/official strategies, choose `*-stable` / `*-official` directly

- `doubao-seedance-1-0-lite-t2v-250428-official`
- `doubao-seedance-1-0-lite-i2v-250428`
- `doubao-seedance-1-0-lite-i2v-250428-official`
- `doubao-seedance-1-0-pro-250528`

Recommendations:
- For text-to-video, prefer `*-t2v-*` or `pro / fast`
- For image-to-video, prefer `*-i2v-*`

### 4.4 Grok video series

Public model:
- `grok-video-3`

Common parameters:
- `prompt`
- `ratio` / `aspect_ratio`
- `resolution` / `size`
- `seconds` / `duration`
- `image` / `image_url` / `input_reference`
- `reference_images`

### 4.5 Kling primary video model

Public base model:
- `kling-video`

Required:
- `model`
- `model_name`

Supported `model_name`:
- `kling-v1`
- `kling-v1-5`
- `kling-v1-6`
- `kling-v2-master`
- `kling-v2-1`
- `kling-v2-1-master`
- `kling-v2-5-turbo`
- `kling-v2-6`
- `kling-v3`

Common `mode`:
- `t2v`
- `i2v`
- `multi_i2v`
- `extend`

Minimum input parameters:
- Text-to-video: `model + model_name + prompt + mode=t2v`
- Image-to-video: `model + model_name + prompt + mode=i2v + image`
- Multi-image reference: `model + model_name + prompt + mode=multi_i2v + image_list`
- Video extension: `model + model_name + mode=extend + video_id`

Example:

```json
{
"model": "kling-video",
"model_name": "kling-v2-6",
"mode": "t2v",
"prompt": "海边日落镜头，电影感，风吹长发",
"duration": 5,
"aspect_ratio": "16:9"
}
```

- `mode=t2v`
- Minimum input: `model + prompt`

- `mode=i2v`
- Minimum input: `model + prompt + image_url`

- `mode=i2v_first_last`
- Minimum input: `model + prompt + image_url + end_image_url`

- `mode=reference_images`
- Minimum input: `model + prompt + reference_images`

- `mode=reference_material`
- Minimum input: `model + prompt + (image_urls/video_urls/audio_urls 至少一种)`

model.description: Model name. Different models trigger different upstream adaptation logic.

prompt.description: Unified prompt entry point. Required for most models.

image.description: Single image entry point; will be mapped to `images` in certain compatibility modes.

duration.description: Unified duration parameter. Some models also accept `seconds`.

metadata.description: Dynamic extension field container. Numerous model-specific fields are deserialized from here.

mode.description: Channel/mode prompt fields, such as i2v_first and reference_images.

images.description: Unified entry point for multiple images. Used by Vidu, Seedance, Veo, etc.

size.description: Unified size input, which maps to resolution/aspect_ratio and related fields.

seconds.description: Sora compatibility entry point; at runtime it falls back to duration.

input_reference.description: Sora/Veo-compatible reference image input. Can be a multipart file or a compatible object.

metadata.description: Dynamic extension field container. Numerous model-specific fields are deserialized from here.

mode.description: Channel/mode prompt fields, such as i2v_first and reference_images.

images.description: Unified entry point for multiple images. Used by Vidu, Seedance, Veo, etc.

size.description: Unified size input, which maps to resolution/aspect_ratio and related fields.

seconds.description: Sora compatibility entry point; at runtime it falls back to duration.

input_reference.description: Sora/Veo-compatible reference image input. Can be a multipart file or a compatible object.

status.description: Should use VideoStatus constants: VideoStatusQueued, VideoStatusInProgress, VideoStatusCompleted, VideoStatusFailed

get.description:
OpenAI compatible video task status query interface.

Returns detailed status information for the video task.

video_url.description: Video result URL. Different models may return either `video_url` or `url`.

url.description: Video result URL. Different models may return either `url` or `video_url`.

error.description: Error message returned when the request fails.

get.description:
Retrieves the video file content of a completed video task.

This endpoint returns the video file stream via proxy.

404.description: Video does not exist or is not completed

post.description:
This series supports the OpenAI video generation format (see [Compatible Formats for Video Generation Sora](/api-reference/en/video-generation-sora-compatible-format/create-video) for details).

Use the Kling model to generate videos from text descriptions.

Supported models: kling-v1, kling-v1-5, etc.

get.description:
This series supports the OpenAI video generation format (see [Compatible Formats for Video Generation Sora](/api-reference/en/video-generation-sora-compatible-format/create-video) for details).

Query the status and results of the Kling text-to-video task.

post.description:
This series supports the OpenAI video generation format (see [Compatible Formats for Video Generation Sora](/api-reference/en/video-generation-sora-compatible-format/create-video) for details).

Generate videos from images using the Kling model.

Supports passing image URLs or Base64-encoded image data via the `image` parameter.

get.description:
This series supports the OpenAI video generation format (see [Compatible Formats for Video Generation Sora](/api-reference/en/video-generation-sora-compatible-format/create-video) for details).

Query the status and results of the Kling image-to-video task.

post.description:
This series supports the OpenAI video generation format (see [Compatible Formats for Video Generation Sora](/api-reference/en/video-generation-sora-compatible-format/create-video) for details).

Used to create video generation tasks and query status and results via task ID.

Core fields for task creation:
- `model`: Required. Model name.
- `prompt`: Required. Primary description for video generation.
- `content`: Optional. Multimedia input array for advanced mode.
- `function_mode`: Optional. Commonly used for advanced modes such as Omni-Reference.

Compatible input parameters:
- `mode`：`t2v / i2v / i2v_first_last / reference_images / reference_material`
- `image_url / image_urls / input_reference`
- `end_image_url / last_image_url`
- `video_urls / audio_urls`
- `seconds / duration`
- `aspect_ratio / ratio / size`
- `quality / resolution`
- `fps`
- `generate_audio / watermark`

Call details:
- When using `mode=reference_material` or `function_mode=omni_reference`, it is recommended to pass text and reference materials via `content`.
- Media fields in `content` support passing URL strings directly as well as object notation, such as `{ "url": "https://example.com/ref.mp4" }`.
- `ratio`, `duration`, `resolution`, and `fps` will affect the final generation configuration.

Key fields in query results:
- `items[].status`
- `items[].content.video_url` or `items[].video_url`
- `items[].error`
- `items[].progress`

Error fields: `error.code`, `error.message`.

```json
{
"error": {
"code": "invalid_request_error",
"message": "requires at least one image in `images`"
}
}
```

model.description: Required. The name of the model.

prompt.example: 参考素材生成一段剧情短视频
prompt.description: Required. The primary description for video generation; cannot be empty.

content.description: Optional. An array of multimedia inputs in advanced mode; recommended for complex reference scenarios.

function_mode.description: Optional. Advanced feature mode, such as `omni_reference` for all-in-one reference.

mode.description: Optional. Compatibility mode fields, such as `t2v`, `i2v`, `i2v_first_last`, `reference_images`, and `reference_material`.

image_url.description: Optional. URL of a single reference image.

image_urls.description: Optional. A list of URLs for multiple reference images.

input_reference.description: Optional. Compatible with reference input fields.

end_image_url.description: Optional. The URL of the last-frame reference image.

last_image_url.description: Optional. URL alias for the last frame reference image.

video_urls.description: Optional. List of reference video URLs.

audio_urls.description: Optional. List of reference audio URLs.

seconds.description: Optional. Video duration in seconds; compatible with `duration`.

duration.description: Optional. Video duration in seconds; compatible with `seconds`.

ratio.description: Optional. Aspect ratio aliases, such as `16:9`.

size.description: Optional. Field for resolution or dimension compatibility.

watermark.description: Optional. Whether to add a watermark.

example.prompt: 参考素材生成一段剧情短视频

content.text: @角色A 在咖啡馆和 @角色B 聊天，@背景视频 作为场景，配上 @背景音乐 --ratio 16:9 --dur 10

error.description: Error message returned when the request fails.

get.description:
This series supports the OpenAI video generation format (see [Compatible Formats for Video Generation Sora](/api-reference/en/video-generation-sora-compatible-format/create-video) for details).

Used to create video generation tasks and query status and results via task ID.

Core fields for task creation:
- `model`: Required. Model name.
- `prompt`: Required. Primary description for video generation.
- `content`: Optional. Multimedia input array for advanced mode.
- `function_mode`: Optional. Commonly used for advanced modes such as Omni-Reference.

Compatible input parameters:
- `mode`：`t2v / i2v / i2v_first_last / reference_images / reference_material`
- `image_url / image_urls / input_reference`
- `end_image_url / last_image_url`
- `video_urls / audio_urls`
- `seconds / duration`
- `aspect_ratio / ratio / size`
- `quality / resolution`
- `fps`
- `generate_audio / watermark`

Call details:
- When using `mode=reference_material` or `function_mode=omni_reference`, it is recommended to pass text and reference materials via `content`.
- Media fields in `content` support passing URL strings directly as well as object notation, such as `{ "url": "https://example.com/ref.mp4" }`.
- `ratio`, `duration`, `resolution`, and `fps` will affect the final generation configuration.

Key fields in query results:
- `items[].status`
- `items[].content.video_url` or `items[].video_url`
- `items[].error`
- `items[].progress`

Error fields: `error.code`, `error.message`.

```json
{
"error": {
"code": "invalid_request_error",
"message": "requires at least one image in `images`"
}
}
```

error.description: Error message returned when the request fails.

post.description:
This series supports the OpenAI video generation format (see [Compatible Formats for Video Generation Sora](/api-reference/en/video-generation-sora-compatible-format/create-video) for details).

This interface topic page is intended only to explain the Veo native format and the current gateway support status.

For the current Veo series, please use the OpenAI Sora compatibility mode for external requests: `POST /v1/videos`.

The Google native mode path is reserved as: `POST /v1beta/models/{model}:predictLongRunning`. The current gateway has not yet connected to the Veo native task distribution link; therefore, calls using the native `instances/parameters` request body are currently not supported.

prompt.example: A cinematic aerial shot of a modern city at sunrise

referenceImages.description: Optional. 1–3 reference images, using Google native `inlineData` or file URI structures.

instances.prompt: A cinematic aerial shot of a modern city at sunrise

200.description: Reserved success response structure. Currently provided for protocol documentation only; actual calls are not supported.

name.description: The operation name, used for subsequent status queries.

get.description:
This series supports the OpenAI video generation format (see [Compatible Formats for Video Generation Sora](/api-reference/en/video-generation-sora-compatible-format/create-video) for details).

This interface topic page is intended solely to explain the Veo native query protocol.

For the current Veo series, please use the `/v1/videos` unified interface for creation and queries instead.

The following `operation_name` query structure is retained for reserved protocol documentation only and does not currently support actual calls.

parameters.name: operation_name
parameters.description: The complete operation name returned by the creation interface. When calling, `/` must be URL-encoded.

200.description: Reserved query response structure. This is currently for protocol specification only and does not support actual calls.

name.description: The operation name, which is consistent with the `name` returned by the creation API.

done.description: Indicates whether the task is completed.

uri.description: The URL of the generated video.

error.description: Error message returned when the request fails.

tool_call_id.description: Tool call ID (used for tool role messages)

parameters.description: Parameter definitions in JSON Schema format

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort (for models that support reasoning)

input.description: Input content, can be a string or an array of messages
Input content, can be a string or an array of messages

remixed_from_video_id.description: Source video ID (if generated based on another video)

BearerAuth.description:
Use Bearer Token authentication.
Format: `Authorization: Bearer sk-xxxxxx`

SessionAuth.name: session
SessionAuth.description: Session authentication, obtained via the login endpoint.

AccessToken.name: Authorization
AccessToken.description: Access Token authentication. Format: Bearer {access_token}, generated via the `/api/user/token` endpoint.

NewApiUser.name: New-Api-User
NewApiUser.description: User ID request header. This must match the ID of the currently logged-in user and is required when using Session or AccessToken authentication.
